column2: add
room
clara
Rin! Hey, Rin!
Somebody grabs my shoulder and shakes my whole body forcefully.
Clara is in front of me, her face fervent as she shouts.
day
room
Come on, Rin! Say something already!
Yesterday was too much... One of us is gone from the drawer...
room
clara
How can you be okay with this?! I couldn't sleep a wink!
I recall the events of yesterday.
I hadn't told Clara about the reality of this drawer, so she saw a little person disappear without knowing anything in advance.
...Sorry. It must have been really hard on you.
You must have been terrified seeing that. For now, you should rest--
Stop that! That's not what I want to hear right now!
Don't you have anything else to say, Rin? About what you did before Saeko came back yesterday evening.
I saw you hand out that huge cherry, and I watched it get eaten up.
clara
And right after that, Saeko came back, and she took the person who ate the cherry and--and she--
clara
I just can't... How could she e-eat somebody...
clara
Even in my wildest dreams, I'd never think something like this could happen! And even if it did, I thought you'd try to stop it!
But you just stood there, watching without a word...
clara
What's happened to you, Rin?! Come on--say something!
akari
...Shut up.
What?
...Shut up. Why are you so loud? It's morning.
I don't care about your hysterics.
akari
Just turn the volume down. Got it?
clara
clara
What are you--
chio
I've got a thing to say too. Clara, stop making trouble for Rin.
clara
Wha--! Trouble?
chio
Yup. Getting angry and then shouting at Rin because of principles from the outside world... Have you even thought about how Rin feels?
clara
I know Rin's feelings much better than you do! How long do you think I've known him?!
Everyone here is the worst! Awful! You're all crazy!
clara
Rin, you won't tell me the same thing as the rest of them, will you? You wouldn't do that...
Telling me to shut up or that I'm being hysterical--even after something...so awful...happened right before my eyes...
clara
How can you all be okay with this...?
I feel multiple gazes on me. I ponder over what I should say.
clara
First of all, Clara, let me say sorry for not telling you something so important. I should have let you know about the situation yesterday.
I know it was shocking, and I'm sure you can't trust me now, but it's my fault for not telling you about Saeko or this drawer.
I'm sure there's more you want to talk about, but everyone's watching here. Let's talk in private later.
Where nobody else can hear us... Okay?
clara
clara
...Okay. But we have to talk.
With those words, Clara turns on her heels and walks away...
...leaving just Chio and Akari in my sight.
akari
I don't think she'll live long.
akari
I'm heading back to bed. Night.
What? Oh, uh, good night then...
Akari lets out a huge yawn and walks off. A few seconds later, I can hear her breathing peacefully in her sleep.
chio
Rin, I'm going to repeat myself.
Get rid of that Clara girl. And fast.
Forget hysterics. She's attacking you, the supervisor. If you keep her around, she'll start causing problems for everyone else.
chio
And you remember what I told you about Health, right?
Yeah...
To me, that Clara girl looks like she could break anytime. Maybe today.
If you don't send Clara to Saeko today, she might lose all her Health. And then we'll all be killed.
Maybe she was your ex, your girlfriend--but you don't have any memories of that now. There's no reason to treat her differently.
chio
Stop thinking so much. Just get rid of the person who's hurting everyone else, so that we can all live longer. Got it?
Sorry. Just let me think for a bit...
Chio's anger still hasn't calmed as I leave her side. I feel the electric tension in the stagnant air of the drawer.
Though Clara was shocked, I put some distance between us to avoid a fight with the other little people. I can't keep ignoring her. I should go talk to her soon.
I walk away from the others and head toward Clara. She seems just a little less agitated than before.
clara
...Thank you, Rin. You actually came.
clara
Sorry for shouting at you earlier. You were just so calm, even though somebody had died.
I was shocked. I thought you'd become somebody I didn't know while in this drawer.
The Rin I knew was a boy who had a kinder and stronger heart than anyone else, who'd never abandon anybody...
clara
This location won't do. Let's go somewhere with a better view. Would you lead the way, Rin?
A better view? In this drawer?
Yes. I want to talk with you somewhere just a little bit calmer... Can't we?
(Is there a place like that in here...?)
Okay, I'll go look.
face-first
I'm supposed to take Clara somewhere with a better view so we can have a calm talk, but where can I find a place like that in this drawer?
How about this view?
clara
Huh? Here? ...The view doesn't look that great to me.
clara
Rin, you had a good sense for this when you were a kid. Won't you trust yourself and think about this more seriously?
Alright...
back
clara
Excuse me, Rin. Who on earth are you talking to?
clara
You've even forgotten what I look like... You really have changed, Rin.
Sorry, I just made a mistake. Give me a moment--I'll be right there.
back
I take Clara's hand and walk to the wall.
The barren wood-grain wall has one lone stamp stuck on it. The drawing of nature looks almost like a nice view from a little window.
clara
...Lovely. You really do have a good sense for these things.
It doesn't hold up to a real view, but the gentle touch of this forest and river makes me remember that day we spent at Takarayama Park.
Takarayama Park?
day
clara
...Yes. I see you've also forgotten that name.
clara
I was a rather sheltered young lady growing up. I spent every day taking lessons.
Piano, ballet, studying...even horse riding. I was forced to do everything I could to better fit the role of the daughter of a wealthy family.
I know now that I was lucky to be born in my position, but at the time, I was always suffering.
clara
Why couldn't I take detours on the way home and play when I wanted like my classmates? I didn't understand.
clara
One day, after a harsh scolding by my piano tutor, I finally reached my boiling point.
I opened the side door in the rear garden and went outside on my own for the first time. I even left a little note in my room to say I was running away.
But I'd always been driven around by car, even back and forth from school. I didn't recognize any of the roads...
clara
I just walked and walked until I found myself in a large park on a hill--Takarayama Park. And that's where I met you, Rin.
Oh...
When you saw me crying on a bench, you called out to me--it was so sweet.
And after you heard what I had to say, you just stood up. You were still so young then, but you decided to go to my house.
You stood up for me to my parents and the staff, saying that they weren't treating me right, how you felt sorry for me...all while holding my hand.
We kept meeting after that, with you protecting me from any dangers I came up against. You were always my hero, Rin.
Wow...
clara
You were. And with time, our relationship deepened... But then, my family decided that I should go to a high school in Germany.
We spent so many days together then, because we didn't want to split up. Do you remember?
But when I returned to Japan a few days ago, I heard that you'd gone missing...
And that's when I knew. This time, it was my turn to protect you. I would protect you no matter what obstacles I faced.
clara
That's why I'm going to fight Saeko.
I don't care how large she is. No matter how strong they are, the villain always loses. I know that.
clara
I don't know what the other people in here are thinking. I don't even know for certain what you're thinking now.
But there's one thing I am sure about.
clara
Rin, what's happening here isn't normal. I can't just accept this without a fight.
I'll escape from here and help you get back to your kind former self. That's my wish.
clara
I'll do whatever is necessary to make it happen. I don't care about any of the other little people.
clara
So, Rin... Won't you join me?
I return to the middle of the room and look around the edges of the drawer.
Clara has decided to fight Saeko after the shocking events of yesterday. Conversely, Chio and Akari are trying to protect the peace we have now.
Honestly, my current self doesn't know which of them is right. There is a large daikon radish pickle in the middle of the drawer.
I feel like today's decision will be a very important one.
Before I decide everything, I should talk to the other little people once more.
day

Clara wants to defeat Saeko, and Chio and Akari are against her. I need to choose somebody out of these three to offer to Saeko again today. I'll talk to everyone before making this important decision.
I head towards Clara. When she sees me, her expression brightens.
clara
Perfect timing, Rin! I have something I want to ask of you.
You do?
Yes, I want to borrow something in this drawer. My first request for the supervisor.
A request...
(I'm happy she's relying on me, but I'm a bit scared, since she's in the middle of fighting with the other little people.)
I'll listen to your request, but just to check... It's nothing dangerous, right?
clara
Hm? Of course it isn't. Rin, could you bring me a clip?
(Huh? A clip?)
(I don't know what she'll use it for, but it should be fine...)
4a
Clara's request to me, the supervisor, is to bring her a clip. I'll do as she asks, since it doesn't sound dangerous.
4a
clara
Rin, who on earth are you talking to? I asked you to bring me a clip.
clara
Have you truly changed into somebody else? I really do have to defeat Saeko...
clara
Rin, please. Bring me the clip--quick. Do you understand me?
Alright...
back
4a
I pick up the item I chose and approach Clara. Her expression clouds over.
clara
Rin, I told you to bring me a clip.
clara
Have you truly changed into somebody else? I really do have to defeat Saeko...
clara
Rin, please. Bring me the clip--quick. Do you understand me?
Alright...
back
4a
remove
I hold the clip in both hands and approach Clara. She smiles brightly at me.
clara
Wow, thank you! You really brought it to me.
Could you keep holding that half? Keep that looped part still with both hands.
I hold down half of the clip with both hands, just as instructed. Clara quickly takes the other half in her hands.
Okay... Hold on tight.
Clara takes the edge of the clip--the tip of the stick poking out of the loop--with both hands.
clara_making_spear
Then, she uses her whole body to force the metal loop to bend, turning half of the clip into a sharp wire.
Thank you. Now, I'm ready.
Wait! Clara, what are you going to do with that wire?
clara
Hm? You need to ask? I'm obviously making this into a weapon to defeat Saeko.
Defeat...Saeko...?
clara
Yes. Didn't you hear me earlier? I'm going to defeat Saeko and protect who you used to be, Rin.
clara
This is the weapon I'll use. I'll take the wire and stab Saeko's finger when it gets close.
But Saeko's so big. Even if you can stab her finger, it won't be enough...
clara
...Do you have any better ideas?
Wha...
If you have any better ideas, feel free to tell me. What's a sure-fire method to stop that giant body of Saeko's from taking another breath?
clara
Poison would be the most effective. For example, adding a lethal poison or drug to the clothes of the person she'll eat today.
But we don't have anything like that here, do we? So I made a plan with the next best thing.
clara
But I should make this tip pointier, like a needle. Do you have anything hard, Rin? Perhaps I could sharpen this on the wall...
Clara takes her handmade weapon and walks away.
I watch her back as she starts sharpening the wire against the wall. I don't think there's anything I can do to stop her.
I quietly walk toward Chio. She is looking down silently with her brows furrowed.
Hi, Chio...
chio
...What? You want to ask me something?
chio
You were talking with Clara earlier, weren't you? What did you think?
Well, I...
You know she's trying to fight Saeko. But you also know it's pointless--completely reckless.
I've seen many people try the same thing in the past, and they all died meaningless deaths.
chio
We can fight as hard as we want, but we can't even win against a single finger of Saeko's.
chio
Don't get me wrong. I don't hate Clara or anything...though I might be a bit jealous.
chio
But more than that, I'm worried about you.
chio
You don't want to see somebody you like get killed for fighting a pointless fight, right?
chio
And Rin... Do you really want to defeat Saeko?
To be honest, I can't get myself to hate her. Out of all the people I've met until now, she's the most innocent, genuine, and adorable.
Sure, it was surprising seeing people getting eaten at first, but can you really say you still think it's unforgivable?
The laws and rules of the outside world make me feel bad about it, but that's about it.
chio
Honestly, I even enjoy it sometimes when I see Saeko toss somebody in her mouth without a care...
chio
Tell me the truth, Rin. You feel the same way, don't you?
chio
If you do, there's no need to go out of your way to try to defeat Saeko.
What you need to do is protect the peace in this drawer and make whatever decision is the best for Saeko.
You understand, don't you, Rin? Trust your true feelings and make the right choice.
next
back
back
I approach Akari. She woke up at some point. She stares at me as I walk closer.
akari
What? Do you need something?
No...
You don't know who to offer up today? Well, don't choose me.
akari
I'm not going to make anybody hungry with how I look.
akari
I meant that as a joke, but...
akari
What?
...Well.
I think that maybe you don't need to give anybody up.
There's no point in staying alive in here anyway. Might as well...
akari
...Forget it. Maybe I just want to get offed in a weird way.
akari
Seriously, forget it. Just decide what you want to do without my input. I don't care even if we all end up getting killed together.
next
back
back
I've finished talking with everyone.
Clara is making a weapon with the wire to fight Saeko. If I want to fight at her side, I should offer Chio to Saeko.
Chio just wants things to stay the same. If I want to stay alive with her, I should offer Clara to Saeko.
Akari...doesn't seem to want anything. But like she said, her Appeal is low. I probably can't offer her to Saeko.
What should I do? I need to think hard on my decision...
I've finished talking with everyone. Clara is making a weapon to fight with Saeko. Chio wants to keep things the same. Akari is a mystery. I need to choose somebody to offer to Saeko.
chio
...Hey, Rin. What are you doing?
chio
Saeko will want another little person with high Appeal today. You have to feed somebody to satisfy her.
(...She's right. I can't do what Akari said right now.)
(As the supervisor of the little people, I need to gather my resolve and choose somebody today.)
back
remove
I find my resolve and slowly approach Akari.
Sorry, Akari... Could you eat this for me?
akari
Are you sure I should have it?
Er... What do you mean by that?
No...
If that's how it is, it's fine.
Akari brings the pickled daikon to her mouth and bites down. The large pickle is slowly shaved down...
I'm sure you don't need me to tell you this, but I was obviously the wrong choice.
I mean, I don't look even the slightest bit yummy.
It's unfortunate that we'll all have to die here. We've been through a lot.

column1: # アカリにとって漬物の匂いは馴染みがあり、死とも結びついている

column2: I guess my last meal being a pickle is a fitting end...
Rin, thank you for the fun memories. Let's have a nice death together.
I'm still flustered, but just as I try to find out the truth behind Akari's words--
next
remove
day
I find my resolve and slowly approach Chio.
When she sees the food in my hands, her eyes grow wide.
chio
...Rin.
I silently hold out the pickled daikon radish in both hands. The unique and tantalizing sour scent hangs over the drawer.
...Sorry. I'd like you to eat this, Chio.
chio
Chio silently looks back and forth between the pickled daikon and me. Then, she slowly closes her eyes.
chio
I tense up on reflex, thinking she might yell or hit me, but the next moment, Chio is grinning like she always does.
day
...I get it. You're good, Rin.
chio_eating_takuan
Chio opens her mouth wide and chomps down on the yellow pickled daikon. She crunches away at the large pickle...
I had a feeling this would happen ever since that girl came.
chio
You've been glued at the hip since she came here. I guess that's just the regular distance between lovers? I'm jealous.
chio
When you get as old as me, you can just tell what's risky, even looking in from the outside. *sigh* Nothing good comes out of living longer...
chio
I mean it. I never even had the choice to fight against Saeko.
I've seen too much. People disappearing doesn't have much of an impact on me anymore. The need to obey Saeko's been engraved into my brain.
chio
Even now, though I want to support you, all I can think of is how it'll just get us killed...
chio
That's why it's the right decision to take me out here. You really are the best supervisor, Rin.
Sorry. And...
Thank you so much for helping me every day. I'm really...grateful.
chio
...Hehe. Don't look so sad. I don't like it when things get too serious.
Oh, but before your girlfriend gets here...
chio
Want to do it one last time? I'll let you come as many times as you want today.
Just as I'm pondering how to respond to Chio's tasteless suggestion--
next
remove
I find my resolve and slowly approach Clara. When she sees me, she starts to smile.
day
However, her eyes go wide when they notice the food in my hands.
clara
Rin, you can't...
I silently hold out the pickled daikon radish in both hands. The unique and tantalizing sour scent hangs over the drawer.
Here. Clara, I'd like you to eat this pickled daikon.
clara
You can't be serious, Rin...
I told you this morning, didn't I? I saw what happened to the person you gave food to yesterday.
And you're still telling me to eat this? You're telling me to...
...Sorry. If you fight Saeko, we'll all get killed.
This is the only thing I can do to protect everyone in this drawer.
clara
...You're the worst.
clara
room
The worst! I trusted you, Rin! Even if you'd lost your memories, I thought you'd stay the same. I thought you'd understand me.
room
But it looks like it was all a misunderstanding on my end. You changed here!
room
They took advantage of your kindness and broke you! The way you think is all wrong!
I get it. Just eat.
clara
I push Clara up against the wall. We're just about the same height.
Eat this. Come on, Clara. It's a request from the person you've liked for so long.
No--stop! Let me go--
Our eyes meet.
rin_kissing_clara_1
I force open Clara's mouth and slip my tongue in. I push the thin pieces of pickled daikon deep into her mouth.
Gulp. Clara's throat moves, and the liquid goes down. She must have had to swallow because her mouth was filled with saliva and my tongue.
rin_kissing_clara_2
It keeps going. Another bite, and another. Chew, then push. My chin and lips hurt, but this is the only way.
As this continues, Clara's eyelids grow heavy. The strength leaves her body, her mouth goes slack, and she swallows the food without resisting.
rin_kissing_clara_3
After it's all over, there is no more pickled daikon in the room...and Clara lies motionless on the floor.
...Well? Are you full now?
She doesn't answer my question. Her breaths are ragged, her chest heaving.
As I watch her, I wipe the saliva stuck to the edge of my lips with the expensive-looking fabric she's wearing. It'll be gone soon anyway.
Now then...
Just as I'd taken a moment to brush the dust off my clothes--
next
room
A low, muffled vibration reverberates from a distance. The sound of heavy footsteps.
room
There's no mistaking it. Saeko has returned to her room.
room
Saeko walks through the room with even breaths. Every step she takes shakes the drawer like an earthquake...
room
Each tremor growing larger and more violent than the last.
room
Soon, they become too strong for me to stand.
I find myself crouching on the floor.
Next, I hear the creak of something moving.
Then, a tremendous commotion that shakes the whole room...
...as the room we're in--the second drawer of a desk--is pried open by Saeko's fingers.
day
shift
open_sliding_in
serious
Hello, cute little people. Were you good today while I was away?
Now then...
serious
I'll enjoy another one of you today.
serious

column2: lick
serious
Saeko's hand comes down into the drawer.
Her unthinkably large fingers fill our vision.
They wander for just a moment, as if carefully making a decision...
And then return to the sky, empty-handed.
serious
...*sigh*
Nobody's making my mouth water today.
...Hey, Rin.
I suppose you were just an ordinary human after all.
...Whatever. I don't need any of you anymore.
gameover
GAME OVER
And then, they stop. Right in front of Chio.
...Looks like the time's finally come.
serious
Her gigantic fingers wrap around Chio's tiny arms and legs...
serious
...and carry her up into the sky.
Saeko, thank you for everything.
serious
And...
serious
Bye, Rin. Keep living for my sake too.

But it is what it is.
serious

Do you think you can get away with this?
You ate a living being! A human with a life, just like you!
And you're even enjoying the cruelty of it--you're a terrible human being!

*sigh* Now my mood is ruined.

The moon's really bright tonight.
Let's go enjoy the night breeze.
night

It's too bad she didn't have the energy to fight back...
smile
But I'm just happy you got her all ready for me, Rin.
